en.name: Carbon dioxide partial pressure in venous blood
en.longDescription: ## Definition & Clinical Context The venous partial pressure of carbon dioxide (PvCO2) is the pressure of CO2 dissolved in venous blood. It is typically 4-6 mmHg higher than arterial PCO2 in stable patients (reflecting tissue CO2 production), but the gradient widens substantially in low-flow states (shock, severe heart failure) [1]. The venous-arterial gradient (`PvCO2 − PaCO2`, also called Pv-aCO2) is used in intensive care as a marker of tissue perfusion: a wide gradient (> 6 mmHg) suggests inadequate cardiac output relative to tissue CO2 production [1]. The source data will typically be a single numeric result per patient-timestamp, produced by a blood gas analyser on a venous sample. Results are reported in mmHg (US convention) or kPa (SI / European convention) [2]. **Note: arterial vs venous.** This concept set covers **venous PCO2 only** (peripheral venous, central venous, mixed venous, capillary blood). Arterial PCO2 is captured by the separate concept set "Carbon dioxide partial pressure in arterial blood". **Note: venous specimens.** Central venous PCO2 (CVC) reflects upper-body return, mixed venous PCO2 (pulmonary artery catheter) reflects whole-body return, peripheral venous PCO2 reflects the drained tissue bed. The user should preserve the specimen distinction in analyses where the source-of-blood matters. **Note: temperature-corrected variant.** Modern blood gas analysers measure at 37 °C. A separate LOINC code exists for PCO2 corrected to the patient's actual temperature (relevant in hypothermia). Both forms are included here; analyses that need to distinguish them must use the LOINC code, not the value. **Note: do not confuse with total CO2.** LOINC distinguishes `Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure]` (PvCO2, dissolved gas, mmHg or kPa) from `Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume]` (total CO2 content, mmol/L). The latter is essentially the bicarbonate plus dissolved CO2 and belongs with the separate concept set "Bicarbonate in blood". Only `[Partial pressure]` codes are included here. ## Included Concepts The set is anchored on four LOINC Hierarchy concepts (scoped to `Chemistry - non-challenge`), each expanded to include all their descendants, with total-CO2 codes excluded by name. The set resolves to eight LOINC "Lab Test" concepts, grouped here by specimen. ### Venous blood - *3021447 / 2021-4 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Venous blood (LOINC)* - *3041290 / 40619-9 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Venous blood (LOINC)* ### Mixed venous blood - *3021513 / 19212-0 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)* - *3029306 / 50981-0 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Mixed venous blood (LOINC)* ### Central venous blood - *1616989 / 97538-3 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Central venous blood (LOINC)* - *1616289 / 97539-1 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Central venous blood (LOINC)* ### Capillary blood - *3023024 / 2020-6 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Capillary blood (LOINC)* - *3042462 / 40620-7 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] adjusted to patient's actual temperature in Capillary blood (LOINC)* **Note on units.** Recommended unit follows LOINC's `EXAMPLE_UCUM_UNITS`: **`mm[Hg]`** (concept 8876 — millimetre of mercury). Conversion: `1 kPa ≈ 7.50 mmHg`. ## Excluded Concepts The following LOINC concepts that exist for CO2 in non-arterial blood are deliberately not part of this set, grouped by reason for exclusion. ### Total CO2 — different measurand (bicarbonate equivalent) - *3010140 / 2027-1 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Venous blood* - *3049133 / 48391-7 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Venous blood by calculation* - *1617274 / 97545-8 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Central venous blood* - *3018225 / 19223-7 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Mixed venous blood* - *40761029 / 57920-1 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Mixed venous blood by calculation* - *3009293 / 16551-4 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood* - *3038734 / 51781-3 — Carbon dioxide, total [Moles/volume] in Capillary blood by calculation* ## Mapping Notes - **Default concept** when the source reports a venous PCO2 without further specification: `3021447 / 2021-4 — Carbon dioxide [Partial pressure] in Venous blood`. - **Source explicitly mentions central venous or mixed venous**: map to the matching LOINC code rather than the generic venous one. - **Source reports PCO2 from arterial blood**: do **not** map. Use "Carbon dioxide partial pressure in arterial blood" instead. - **Source reports "total CO2" or "TCO2" (mmol/L)**: do **not** map. Use the "Bicarbonate in blood" concept set. - **Do not confuse with**: arterial PCO2 (separate concept set), bicarbonate (separate concept set), end-tidal CO2. ## References [1] Vallet B, Pinsky MR, Cecconi M. Resuscitation of patients with septic shock: please "mind the gap"! *Intensive Care Med*. 2013;39(9):1653-5. Available: https://doi.org/10.1007/s00134-013-2998-5 [2] Nomenclature for Properties and Units (NPU). IFCC/IUPAC. NPU codes (current release). Available: https://npu-terminology.org/npu-database/
